_comment: Uses the 'command' runner (not a Vitest-specific one) because Angular 22's native @angular/build:unit-test builder owns Vitest internally and doesn't expose a standalone vitest.config.ts for Stryker's coverage-analysis runners to drive directly - see stryker-mutator.io's Angular guide, which only documents Karma/Jest. The command runner is slower (no per-mutant test filtering, every mutant re-runs the whole suite via `npm test`) but works reliably regardless of the underlying test builder. This is opt-in, release-gate tooling - run explicitly (`npm run test:mutation`), not part of every `npm test`.
